steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: We need the choice that best describes the overall purpose of the passage — what it sets out to do structurally and argumentatively.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'present a disagreement between scholars': The passage does more than note a disagreement; it explains the methodological reason behind it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'present and account for a potentially misleading characteristic of historians' work': The passage identifies that historians implicitly portray capitalism as imposed on Africa, then explains this stems from archival gaps — not bias. That's exactly presenting and accounting for a misleading characteristic. ✓
  • Choice C — 'focus on Africanist scholars': Africanist scholars are mentioned to establish the contrast, but they are not the passage's focus. ✗
  • Choice D — 'misframe the problem as one of data interpretation': The passage's point is data availability, not interpretation — and the passage never claims historians are misinterpreting data. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
